name: repo_scanner
description:
Scans local source files for leaked secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys), PII (emails, SSNs, credit cards), and missing .gitignore protections. Works on any language — scans file content, not package metadata.
Use when: user asks about hardcoded credentials, leaked tokens, .env exposure, or PII in source files.
Scope: local directory — no network access, no GitHub API.
Not for: package vulnerabilities (use dependency_audit), code quality issues (use python_lint_checker), or GitHub code scanning findings from a remote repo (use code_scanning_alerts).

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the directory to scan. Example: '/Users/me/my-project'

checks.description: Comma-separated checks to run: secrets, gitignore, pii. Default: 'all'. Example: 'secrets,gitignore'
